slides.headline: Jack-Tar Deckhand: Your AI Crew for Conference-Quality Decks
body_points: How an AI-First Skill Suite Turns Ideas into Presentations
slides.visual_direction: Nautical title card with ship's wheel or brass compass motif against deep navy background, gold accents, parchment texture

slides.headline: Presentations Are the Dark Matter of Business Communication
body_points: Hours spent arranging slides instead of thinking about what to say
Design inconsistency, image hunting, copy-paste from old decks
Speaker notes as an afterthought — or not at all
The result: mediocre decks that don't serve the speaker or the audience
slides.visual_direction: Structured storm scene — scattered presentation slides caught in wind, a clock showing wasted time, a frustrated figure at a desk. Dark, dramatic atmosphere.

slides.headline: One Brief In, Conference-Quality Deck Out
body_points: You provide a talk brief: topic, audience, duration, tone
The system produces a complete .pptx with narrative structure and branded visuals
AI-generated images, intelligent data graphics, and timed speaker notes
All derived from your corporate identity and calibrated to your talk duration
slides.visual_direction: Calm nautical scene — clear horizon, calm sea at golden hour, suggesting clarity after the storm of slide 2

slides.headline: 13 Steps from Brief to Deck
body_points: Validate Brief → Brand Manager → Slide Stylist → Narrative Architect
SmartArt Selector → Strategy Map → SmartArt Extractor
Speaker Notes Writer → Image Generation Bridge → SmartArt Renderer
Chart Renderer → Deck Assembler → Deck QA
slides.visual_direction: Pipeline flowchart showing the complete system
slides.visual_intent: Show the full pipeline as a flowchart

slides.headline: Built for Speakers Who Think, Not Designers Who Click
body_points: Set A: Conference Speakers — need quality fast, focus on delivery not design
Set B: Technical Leaders — need architecture diagrams, data visualisations, system overviews
Set C: Business Presenters — need branded slides, chart-heavy decks, professional polish
Shared: Anyone who has content but not design time
slides.visual_direction: Three overlapping audience groups with distinctive icons for each
slides.visual_intent: Show the target audiences as a Venn diagram with AI-generated icons for each audience

slides.headline: How Jack-Tar Compares
body_points: Features: Narrative Intelligence, Image Gen, Brand, SmartArt, Notes, Assembly, QA
Jack-Tar: Yes, Yes, Yes, Yes, Yes, Yes, Yes
Manual PowerPoint: No, No, Manual, No, Manual, No, No
Canva AI: No, Yes, Partial, No, No, No, No
Beautiful.ai / Gamma.app: Partial capability across some dimensions
slides.visual_direction: Clean comparison grid showing competitive advantage

slides.headline: SWOT: Where Jack-Tar Stands
body_points: Strengths: Deep narrative intelligence, 6 AI personas working in concert, 3 rendering engines for data graphics, Full brand awareness and enforcement
Weaknesses: Requires Claude Code environment, No graphical user interface, Learning curve for talk brief specification
Opportunities: Enterprise deck automation at scale, Conference speaker productivity tools, Education and training content generation
Threats: Native AI features arriving in PowerPoint and Google Slides, Canva's growing API ecosystem, Open-source slide generation tools
slides.visual_direction: Strategic SWOT analysis with nautical themed AI rendering
slides.visual_intent: SWOT analysis of the Jack-Tar platform rendered as a full AI image

slides.headline: Six Dimensions of Deck Quality
body_points: Narrative Structure: 9, Visual Design: 8, Image Quality: 8
Data Visualisation: 9, Brand Consistency: 9, Speaker Support: 7
slides.visual_direction: Radar chart showing capability scores across six dimensions
slides.visual_intent: Radar chart comparing Jack-Tar capabilities across 6 dimensions

slides.headline: How It Works: Watch the Pipeline Build This Deck
body_points: Compass — Navigate the brief
Telescope — Discover the brand
Ship's Wheel — Steer the narrative
Map — Chart the visuals
slides.visual_direction: Individual nautical elements — brass compass, telescope, ship's wheel, aged map — each generated separately and placed at exact positions with labels

slides.headline: Every Deck Starts with a Brand
body_points: The brand-manager accepts logos, PDF guidelines, .pptx templates, or manual hex input
It extracts a reusable BrandProfile that persists across deck sessions
This deck: navy #1B3A4B, gold #C67B2F, parchment #F5F0E8, Playfair Display headings
Nautical illustration style — aged maritime maps, brass instruments, rope textures
This slide's styling is proof the system works
slides.visual_direction: Clean composed layout showing the brand profile data

slides.headline: The AI Doesn't Just Arrange Slides — It Thinks About Story
body_points: Is the talk persuasive? Yes: hook-body-callback-cta
Is it problem-solving? Yes: situation-complication-resolution
Is it historical? Yes: chronological
Is it a technical deep-dive? Yes: deep-dive
Is it a comparison? Yes: compare-contrast
slides.visual_direction: Decision tree showing narrative arc selection logic
slides.visual_intent: Show the decision tree for narrative arc selection

slides.headline: The Selector Sees Structure Where You See Bullets
body_points: Features: Content Pattern, Graphic Type, Engine
Sequential steps: Flowchart, Mermaid. Decisions: Decision Tree, Mermaid. Schedule: Gantt, Mermaid
Values: Bar Chart, Vega-Lite. Trends: Line Chart, Vega-Lite. Multi-axis: Radar, Vega-Lite
SWOT: Custom SVG. Matrix: Custom SVG. Venn: Custom SVG. Timeline: Custom SVG. Funnel: Custom SVG
slides.visual_direction: Content trigger to graphic type mapping table
slides.visual_intent: Show the 10 graphic types mapped to their content triggers as a feature matrix

slides.headline: From Prose to Structured Data — No LLM Required
body_points: The extractor is pure Python — regex and string parsing, not AI reasoning
Deterministic, testable, and fast — 'Q1: 120' becomes Vega-Lite, 'Research, Design' becomes Mermaid
'Strengths: Brand, Team' becomes a SWOT quadrant data structure
Overflow handling: truncate, paginate, summarise, or reject when data exceeds capacity
slides.visual_direction: Clean composed layout explaining the extraction process

slides.headline: One Data Structure, Three Ways to Draw It
body_points: Set A: Mermaid.js — flowcharts, decision trees, Gantt charts
Set B: Vega-Lite — bar charts, line charts, radar charts
Set C: Custom SVG — SWOT, matrices, Venn, timelines, funnels
Shared A+C: Flowcharts can render via both Mermaid and Custom SVG
Shared B+Matplotlib: Comparator runs both for bar/line charts
slides.visual_direction: Three-circle Venn diagram showing engine capabilities and overlaps
slides.visual_intent: Show the three rendering engines as a Venn diagram

slides.headline: Draft Phase: Compete. Production Phase: Execute.
body_points: All Eligible Engines Render: 100
Image Reviewer Scores Each: 60
Winner Selected by Score: 30
Production Renders Winner Only: 10
slides.visual_direction: Funnel showing the narrowing from multiple engine candidates to single winner

slides.headline: From Clean SVG to Full AI Render
body_points: T0 Pure Programmatic: Clean SVG styled with design tokens — free, fast, data-precise
T1 AI Background: Programmatic graphic over AI-generated atmospheric image — adds visual depth
T2 AI Elements: Programmatic structure with AI-generated icons per node — adds meaning
T3 Full AI Render: Entire graphic rendered as an AI image — maximum visual impact
slides.visual_direction: Timeline showing enrichment progression from simplest to richest
slides.visual_intent: Show the enrichment tiers as a timeline from simplest to richest

slides.headline: Local Draft, Cloud Production — Try Cheap First
body_points: Ollama Local Draft: 1000
Cloud Low-Tier 720p: 500
Cloud Full-Tier 2K+: 100
Production Approved: 20
slides.visual_direction: Pipeline funnel showing render stages narrowing from many drafts to few production renders
slides.visual_intent: Show the render funnel stages as a pipeline funnel

slides.headline: 35 Automated Checks Before Any Human Sees It
body_points: Contrast: 4, Margins: 3, Text Overflow: 3, Consistency: 5
Image Quality: 4, Placeholders: 2, Missing Content: 3
Accessibility: 3, Keynote: 3, SmartArt: 5
slides.visual_direction: Bar chart showing QA check counts by category
slides.visual_intent: Show the QA categories as a bar chart

slides.headline: Not One AI — A Crew
body_points: Deck Conductor: Brief Validation through Final Delivery
SmartArt Selector: Selection phase. Prompt Engineer: Image Generation
Image Reviewer: Quality Gate. Presentation Reviewer: Final Review
slides.visual_direction: Gantt chart showing when each AI persona is active in the pipeline
slides.visual_intent: Show the persona activity phases as a Gantt chart

slides.headline: From 3 Sentences to 28 Slides in Minutes
body_points: 28 slides, 1 nautical brand, 18 SmartArt graphics across 3 engines
4 enrichment tiers demonstrated, 6 rendering strategies exercised
35 QA checks passed — this deck was built by the system it describes
slides.visual_direction: Clean stat callout layout with key numbers highlighted

slides.headline: Why This Architecture Wins
body_points: The strategic advantages of AI-First presentation engineering
slides.visual_direction: Treasure map scene with figurative landmarks — a compass rose, dotted trail, X-marks-the-spot. Section title positioned by vision analysis against the map background.

slides.headline: 33 Services, 6 Personas, 60 Interactions
body_points: Built on AI-First Business Service Architecture: 4 L1 domains
Content, Design, Image, Assembly & QA — each owns its decisions
The narrative-architect owns story, slide-stylist owns visuals, imagegen-bridge owns routing
Every service independently invocable — not just a pipeline
slides.visual_direction: Clean composed layout — could embed the L1 architecture SVG diagram

slides.headline: Conference Quality Doesn't Mean Conference Budget
body_points: Ollama Drafts: 0, Cloud Drafts: 0.50, Production Images: 1.50
SmartArt Rendering: 0, QA and Review: 0
Total Typical Deck: 2.00
slides.visual_direction: Bar chart showing cost breakdown by category in USD
slides.visual_intent: Show costs by category as a bar chart

slides.headline: Three Layers of Review, Zero Tolerance for Mediocrity
body_points: Image Reviewer — per-image quality gate with 5 criteria
Deck QA — 35 automated anti-pattern checks with max 2 correction cycles
Presentation Reviewer — narrative coherence, visual storytelling, pacing, audience fit
slides.visual_direction: Three-step flowchart showing the quality gate pipeline
slides.visual_intent: Show the quality pipeline as a flowchart

slides.headline: Six Ways to Build a Slide
body_points: Composed: Standard PptxGenJS shapes and text. SmartArt: Data-driven graphics from 3 engines
Background: AI atmospheric image with text zones. Backdrop: Vision-detected positioning
Pragmatic Composition: Individual AI elements at exact coordinates
Full Render: Entire slide as a single AI-generated image
slides.visual_direction: Timeline showing the 6 rendering strategies from simplest to most complex
slides.visual_intent: Show the 6 rendering strategies as a timeline from simplest to most complex

slides.headline: From Research to 603 Tests in 6 Days
body_points: 11 implementation phases from foundation to SmartArt
Cumulative test count grew from 38 to 603
AI-First SDLC with TDD, subagent-driven development, and continuous architecture
slides.visual_direction: Line chart showing cumulative test count growth across project phases
slides.visual_intent: Show the project phases as a line chart tracking cumulative tests over time

slides.headline: What's Next
body_points: Phase A SmartArt v1: Now to Q2 2025
Phase B Complex Diagrams: Q2 to Q3 2025
Phase C Brand Kit Integration: Q3 to Q4 2025
Phase D Visual Regression: Q4 2025 to Q1 2026
slides.visual_direction: Gantt chart showing the four development phases with time ranges
slides.visual_intent: Show the roadmap as a Gantt chart

slides.headline: Set Sail. Your Deck Awaits.
body_points: Jack-Tar Deckhand — AI-First Presentation Engineering
